findings.title: Insecure Secret Management
findings.description: Task 7 provisions S3 credentials via a standard Kubernetes Secret. If these manifests are stored in version control, it risks credential leakage. There is also no mechanism defined for secret rotation.
findings.recommendation: Implement a secure secret management solution such as External Secrets Operator, HashiCorp Vault, or Sealed Secrets to prevent plaintext credentials in repositories.

findings.title: Unenforced Internal Encryption in Transit
findings.description: Task 9 sets up connection strings for PostgreSQL and Redis. There is no explicit requirement to enforce TLS (sslmode=require or rediss://) for internal pod-to-database communication.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the ConfigMap connection strings enforce TLS/SSL for both PostgreSQL and Redis, and verify the operators are configured to require encrypted connections.

findings.title: Missing Pod Security Contexts
findings.description: The deployment tasks for operators and databases do not specify Pod Security Standards (PSS) such as running as non-root, dropping ALL capabilities, or using read-only root filesystems.
findings.recommendation: Enforce restricted Pod Security Standards at the namespace level and explicitly configure securityContexts in the deployment manifests to run as non-root.

findings.title: Lack of Security Logging and Monitoring
findings.description: The plan mentions monitoring in scaling tasks, but lacks foundational tasks to deploy a logging/monitoring stack or enable database audit logging (e.g., pgAudit).
findings.recommendation: Add a task to deploy a centralized logging solution and enable audit logging extensions (like pgAudit) on the PostgreSQL cluster to track sensitive data access.

findings.title: Unpinned Helm Charts and Images
findings.description: Tasks deploying operators and databases do not explicitly require pinning Helm chart versions or container image digests, which could lead to unexpected upstream changes or supply chain attacks.
findings.recommendation: Update deployment tasks to strictly pin Helm chart versions and use immutable image digests (SHA256) instead of mutable tags like '7.2-alpine'.

summary: The infrastructure plan demonstrates a strong baseline with logical namespace isolation, network policies, and automated backups. However, it lacks secure secret management, explicit internal TLS enforcement, and pod security standards, which introduces moderate risk to the deployment.
strengths: Logical namespace separation between databases and application services.
Implementation of Network Policies from the start, with plans to refine them for least privilege.
Automated backups and WAL archiving planned for PostgreSQL.
Use of cert-manager for automated TLS certificate lifecycle management.
Integration of a Web Application Firewall (WAF) via Cloudflare.
